ant.lesson: A 'no ad-hoc font-size' token guard must distinguish an ABSOLUTE magic number (px/rem) from an INTENTIONAL relative unit (em in @media-print ::after URL-append, which must scale to whatever link it trails). Exempt-AND-lock (whitelist the exact 2 rules + assert they live in their print ::after context), never blanket-fail on all units.

Core Web Vitals (LCP/CLS/INP) are NOT measurable without Lighthouse in the build sandbox. A local perf PR can only verify format-wiring correctness (WebP/<picture>/image-set/tokens); the actual number is a deploy-time producer-read-back. Queue it as a tracked DEPLOY.md checklist item — never fake a score.

human.observation: A 'no ad-hoc value' regression test can be silently reverted by a cron generator that owns part of the HTML (wire_letter_writers.py STYLE_BLOCK). The guard must ALSO assert the generator emits the tokenized form, else the daily cron run re-introduces the magic numbers and the lock is theater.

eagle.observation: Kleiber clearing the WEB-QUALITY lens ('CLEAR TO PUSH BOTH') is NOT authorization for the irreversible PUSH — the push is a separate Jeff-gated Tier-2 action bundled with the hosting deploy. Held the push correctly; a quality-gate clearance and an irreversible-action approval are different gates.

ant.lesson: An automated deploy's read-back finding must ride the LOG, not the exit code (R2 exit-code-outcome-mismatch generalizes to deploy): CDN propagation lag is NOT an execution failure, so exit 0 on push-success + record readback-pending; a nonzero would make launchd read a lagging-but-successful deploy as a crash.

A deploy-test must NEVER fire a real force-push: redirect state+log to a tmp dir + a dry-run env flag that stubs the network, so every path (disarmed/change-detect/gate/deploy) is exercised with zero prod side effect (test-fired-prod-side-effect guard).

Change-detect BEFORE the expensive gate: a canonical sha256 compare is cheap, sync_publish+pytest is not; gate only when there IS a change to ship, so a no-op weekly run costs one shasum not a full test run.

human.observation: Source-of-truth for a DERIVED serving artifact: a gh-pages snapshot is not source, so it can legitimately carry the live working-tree state (incl. an always-uncommitted cron diff) WITHOUT forcing the cron to commit. The 'which state do we deploy' tension dissolves once you name the artifact derived-not-source.

eagle.observation: Session-start briefing said 'no PENDING items' but a grep of the IR found a live approved increment (auto-redeploy). Verifying the surface-claim before trusting the briefing's queue summary is what kept the queue from going idle prematurely.
